content.problemStatement:
You are given an array AAA of NNN elements, such that 1≤Ai≤N1 \le A_i \le N1≤Ai​≤N for all 1≤i≤N1 \leq i \leq N1≤i≤N.

Count the number of permutation†^{\dag}† pairs (P,Q)(P, Q)(P,Q) where:


PPP and QQQ are permutations of length NNN;
Ai=max⁡(Pi,Qi)A_i = \max(P_i, Q_i)Ai​=max(Pi​,Qi​) for all 1≤i≤N1 \le i \le N1≤i≤N.
Since the number can be huge, print it modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.

†^{\dag}†A permutation of length NNN consists of all integers from 111 to NNN exactly once.


Input Format

The first line of input will contain a single integer TTT, denoting the number of test cases.
Each test case consists of two lines of input.

The first line of each test case contains a single integer NNN - the size of the array.
The second line contains NNN integers, A1,A2,…,ANA_1, A_2,\ldots, A_NA1​,A2​,…,AN​ - the elements of the array.




Output Format
For each test case, output on a new line, the number of permutation pairs (P,Q)(P, Q)(P,Q) satisfying the condition, modulo 109+710^9 + 7109+7.
constraints: The sum of NNN over all test cases won't exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^53⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111 : There is only 111 valid permutation pair, P=[1,2]P = [1, 2]P=[1,2] and Q=[1,2]Q = [1, 2]Q=[1,2].
Test case 222 : There are 222 valid permutation pairs :

P=[1,2],Q=[2,1]P = [1, 2], Q = [2, 1]P=[1,2],Q=[2,1]
P=[2,1],Q=[1,2]P = [2, 1], Q = [1, 2]P=[2,1],Q=[1,2]
